name: Act on the Protection of Personal Information
description: Japan's Act on the Protection of Personal Information (APPI) — Japan's primary data protection statute, originally enacted 2003, most recently amended 2020/2022 ("Amended Act on the Protection of Personal Information", tentative translation). Organized into 8 chapters: I General Provisions (Arts 1-3), II Responsibilities of Central/Local Governments (Arts 4-6), III Measures Relating to Protection (Arts 7-14), IV Obligations of a Personal Information Handling Business Operator (Arts 15-58-5, in 6 sections covering handling operators, pseudonymously/anonymously processed information operators, supervision, and private-sector promotion), V The Personal Information Protection Commission (Arts 59-74), VI Miscellaneous Provisions (Arts 75-81), VII Penal Provisions (Arts 82-88). Enforced by the PPC.
relationToCloudSecurity: Cloud services handling Japanese residents' personal data must comply, including the security-control obligations in Article 20 ("Security Control Action" — organizational/personnel/physical/technical measures) and the cross-border third-party-provision restrictions in Article 24 ("Restriction on Provision to a Third Party in a Foreign Country"). Note: the base third-party-provision restriction, Article 23, is one of this extraction's known gaps (see scope.known_gaps below).
relationToAISecurity: AI systems that process personal information relating to Japanese residents (training data, inference inputs, profiling) fall within APPI's scope; the PPC has issued supplementary guidance on AI-specific handling that is not yet captured in this extraction (see TODO.md Japan catalog).

license.notes: Japan's Copyright Act (Act No. 48 of 1970), Article 13, excludes laws and regulations — and official government translations thereof — from copyright protection. As primary legislation, APPI's text (including this PPC-published English translation) is not subject to copyright. This is a stronger position than most other Japanese-government documents in this repo's Japan catalog (cf. ISMAP, whose Control Criteria is a program standard rather than a law, and whose license status is unresolved).

links.textUsed: https://www.ppc.go.jp/en/legal/ (English translation PDF, exact revision date not marked on the PDF itself)

lifecycle.notes: Substantially amended 2015, 2020, and 2022 (the source PDF is labeled "Amended Act on the Protection of Personal Information (Tentative Translation)"). A further amendment bill was reported in parliament as of mid-2026 (see TODO.md Japan catalog) — not reflected here.

current_extraction.tool: marker (PDF -> appi.md) + custom Python regex parser (extract_appi.py)
current_extraction.notes: 2026-07-16 rewrite of extract_appi.py: the original parser looked for article titles as text following "Article N" on the same line, but this translation's parenthetical titles (e.g. "(Purpose)") actually PRECEDE "Article N" on the prior line — this misalignment corrupted both the title field (truncated first line of body, not a title) and the content field (missing its first line). Rewritten to search backward from each "Article N" marker for the nearest preceding "(...)" heading. Also recovered: (a) the regex previously required "Article N" to be the only text on its line, missing every article whose body starts on the same line or that's prefixed with a markdown "- " bullet (this covers most of Chapter VII, Arts 82-88); (b) "Article 2" (Definitions) never appears as literal text in the marker output — a source-extraction/OCR gap, not a regex bug — recovered by manual insertion: Chapter I's table of contents states it spans "Articles 1 to 3", Article 1 is Purpose and Article 3 is Overall Vision (both present with correct numbers), so the "(Definition)" block between them is Article 2 by elimination.

extraction_history.reason: Priority-list acquisition (Marker) — title/content field misalignment bug, only 73 of 88 articles captured
Fixed title/content alignment bug and bullet-prefixed article matching; recovered Article 2. 81 of 88 articles now captured (up from 73, several corrupted).

scope.notes: 17 article numbers are missing from this extraction. Some (e.g. 58 vs the '58-2' through '58-5' supplementary articles that ARE present) may be legitimately absent/renumbered in this consolidated text; others (e.g. Article 87, a joint-liability clause whose text appears unmarked as sub-paragraphs under Article 86 in the source markdown — the same kind of OCR/marker gap that affected Article 2) are extraction gaps, not law gaps. Not yet individually verified article-by-article against the official japaneselawtranslation.go.jp text (blocked by that site's bot protection returning HTTP 403 to both WebFetch and curl; would need a JS-capable browser fetch — see TODO.md).

notes: Article-level SecID subpaths added 2026-07-16 for the 81 recovered articles (registry/regulation/jp/go/ppc.json).
